title: Methodology
intro: The platform monitors promises and parliamentary activity using documented logic, official sources, and reproducible metrics.

paragraphs: The goal is to make clearer what was promised, what has progressed, and where documented progress is missing.
civic-pulse does not represent public institutions, political parties, or commercial actors. The platform exists for transparency, comparison, and public verification.
The platform aims for neutrality in description, clarity in categorization, and careful distinction between political statements, official evidence, and editorial interpretation.
Every profile, card, or indicator is meant to be explainable and verifiable. When an official source is missing, the claim should not be treated as a verified fact.
Errors, ambiguities, and updates are handled on a document basis with priority on improving the accuracy of the public archive.
civic-pulse is built by 38Shift, an AI-first engineering and technology consultancy that partners human expertise with machine intelligence to develop advanced digital products.
38Shift works with founders and growth leaders across AI strategy, agent development, product design, and MVP launches, helping organizations evolve into AI-native operations.

steps.title: Data source
steps.text: Promises are taken from official policy and government documents. Parliamentary activity is taken from the official transcripts of the legislature.

steps.title: Progress monitoring
steps.text: Transcripts are structured into a machine-readable format (speaker, text, sessionId, date, source) and linked to the member roster via name normalization.

steps.title: Evaluation methodology
steps.text: For each member the system computes: speech count, words, sessions, and topical mentions across the platform's category taxonomy. The radar map shows the relative weight of topics.

steps.title: Linking promises to top members
steps.text: Each promise links to a primary topic. The detail view shows the top three members for that topic by: mentions > score > speechCount > wordCount.

metrics.title: Speeches
metrics.text: Number of interventions where the member appears as speaker in a transcript.

metrics.title: Spoken words
metrics.text: Sum of words across all interventions by the member (after text normalization).

metrics.title: Sessions
metrics.text: Number of unique sessions where the member made at least one intervention.

metrics.title: Most-discussed topics (radar)
metrics.text: For each topic, keyword mentions are counted; score (%) = topic mentions / total mentions × 100.

ai: Automated agents are used as an engineering layer for building and refreshing the pipeline, rules, and validations. They are not used to invent facts or to replace official sources.
Numeric results come from deterministic scripts over the transcripts and local datasets; each member profile preserves the official transcript source.
noteBody: This is an independent monitoring tool. Data is refreshed as new official evidence becomes available, with accuracy and maximum transparency as the goal.
